forensic_flags.narrative: DPO ~113 days (FY2025) vs a ~30-60 day norm — consistent with supplier finance booked in trade payables. Included in economic debt — behaves like a callable facility.
forensic_flags.pointer: AP footnote; supplier-finance disclosure (ASU 2022-04, ASC 405-50); MD&A liquidity.

forensic_flags.narrative: Cumulative proxy EBITDA $11,829M converted to 59% in OCF ($7,004M). Covenants are EBITDA-based; default is cash-based.
forensic_flags.pointer: CF working-capital lines; receivables securitization / transfer-of-financial-assets footnotes; revenue-recognition policy.

forensic_flags.narrative: Free cash flow -$119M in FY2025 (OCF − capex). Cash covers ~102 months at this burn.
forensic_flags.pointer: MD&A liquidity & capital resources; revolver availability / borrowing base; maturity schedule.

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: Incurrence-based leverage ratios only; no traditional maintenance financial covenant found in the covenant package. The agreement uses a Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio trigger (2.50:1.00) to define an "Amortization Period," and a Combined Net Leverage Ratio as an incurrence test, rather than a maintenance leverage covenant tested each period.
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio of 2.50:1.00 (springing trigger for an "Amortization Period"); Combined Net Leverage Ratio used as an incurrence test under Section 6.04(b). No numerically capped maintenance leverage covenant threshold is stated in the provided text.
ebitda_addback_categories: Restructuring/new product design, development and introduction costs and expenses
Intellectual property development and new systems design costs
IT systems implementation, replacement, development or upgrade costs and technology initiatives
Governmental investigation, litigation or other dispute costs (including customer disputes)
Warranty payments and product recall / field service campaign liabilities

Transaction-related fees, charges, losses and costs (acquisitions, Restricted Payments, Investments, recapitalizations, asset sales, issuances, incurrence/registration/repayment or modification of Indebtedness, equity issuances, refinancings, amendments/waivers) and non-recurring merger costs
Cumulative effect of a change in accounting principles
Non-cash compensation charges from equity-based awards and non-cash deemed finance charges on pension liabilities
One-time non-cash charges or increases in amortization/depreciation from purchase accounting or reorganization/restructuring
Goodwill or other intangible asset impairment charges or write-offs
covenants.restricted_payments_basket_size: Section 6.05 (Limitation on Restricted Payments) governs; specific basket dollar amount not set out in the provided excerpt. Asset Disposition de minimis basket is $5 million per calendar year (clause (g)).
covenants.mfn_sunset_period: Not present in the provided text (no MFN / most-favored-nation pricing protection clause located).
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Notably constrained relative to typical sponsor deals. The agreement contains Section 6.01 "Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises," which is an express anti-LME covenant. The "Designation" defined term refers only to designating the Loan Parties as "unrestricted subsidiaries" under the Existing Debt Documents (i.e., in relation to a separate debt stack), not a broad borrower right to designate unrestricted subsidiaries here. Section 6.17 (Delaware LLC Divisions) further restricts division-type maneuvers. No open-ended unrestricted-subsidiary designation basket is granted in the provided text.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This agreement appears purpose-built to resist liability-management exercises, reflecting its post-Original/A&R restructuring posture. Key protections: (1) Section 6.01 is an express "Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises" — a direct anti-uptier/anti-drop-down covenant, unusual and highly protective; (2) Section 9.23 "Prohibition Regarding Cooperation Agreements" cuts against coordinated lender-group priming maneuvers; (3) Section 6.17 restricts Delaware LLC divisions (anti-Serta/anti-drop-down division play); (4) Section 6.02 "Prohibition on Outside Accounts" and Sections 6.12–6.13 (Impairment of Security Interests; Intercreditor Agreements) tighten collateral leakage. The ABS/InfraCo structure and IP License Agreement (Section 6.15 governs amendments to Shared Services / IP arrangements) is the principal residual value-leakage vector to watch, given prior "Designation" of Loan Parties as unrestricted subsidiaries under Existing Debt Documents. Net: low drop-down/uptier vulnerability by design; the residual risk is structural subordination via the separately-financed ABS Loan Parties and CSC-affiliate IP/shared-services arrangements rather than in-agreement priming.

citation.quote: "Section 6.01. Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises"

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: No maintenance leverage covenant identified in the provided text. The agreement uses incurrence-style ratio tests (Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio, Combined Net Leverage Ratio) rather than a stated maintenance financial covenant. There is a springing "Amortization Period" mechanic tied to the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeding 2.50:1.00, but no maintenance leverage covenant threshold is stated in the excerpted clauses.
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: Not stated as a maintenance covenant. Relevant ratio trigger: Amortization Period commences when Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeds 2.50:1.00 (and ends when it no longer exceeds 2.50:1.00).
ebitda_addback_categories: Business optimization costs, charges or expenses
New product design, development and introduction costs
Intellectual property development and new systems design costs
Operational, reporting and IT systems implementation/replacement/development/upgrade costs and technology initiatives
Costs/expenses/charges relating to any governmental investigation, litigation or other dispute (including with any customer)
Warranty payments and product recall / field service campaign liabilities

Fees, charges, losses, costs and expenses (or amortization thereof) in connection with acquisitions, Restricted Payments, Investments, recapitalizations, asset sales, issuance/incurrence/repayment or modification of Indebtedness, equity issuances, refinancing transactions and related amendments/waivers, and non-recurring merger costs
Cumulative effect of a change in accounting principles
Non-cash equity-based compensation charges (stock, options, other equity awards) and non-cash deemed finance charges on pensions
One-time non-cash charges or increases in amortization/depreciation resulting from purchase accounting (acquisitions, reorganizations, restructurings)
Goodwill or other intangible asset impairment charges or write-offs
covenants.restricted_payments_basket_size: Not specified in the provided excerpts. Section 6.05 ("Limitation on Restricted Payments") is referenced with a Schedule 6.05 of Existing Investments, but no builder/starter basket dollar amount is set out in the text provided.
covenants.mfn_sunset_period: No MFN (most-favored-nation) pricing protection clause or sunset is present in the excerpted text.
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: The provided excerpts do not include an "Unrestricted Subsidiary" definition or a designation mechanic; the structure is built around Loan Parties, Subsidiaries, Group Members and a discrete set of ABS Transaction Parties (a ring-fenced receivables/ABS structure). No unrestricted-subsidiary designation flexibility is evidenced in the text provided.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This is an atypically LME-defensive package. Article VI opens with Section 6.01 "Prohibition on Liability Management Exercises" and Section 6.02 "Prohibition on Outside Accounts," signaling express anti-LME drafting to curb uptier/drop-down structures. Section 3.28 "Material Assets" plus the "Material Assets" definition (any property/assets, including IP, material to the business of the Loan Parties taken as a whole) supports a J.Crew-style asset/IP transfer blocker. Note residual leakage vectors: (i) the ring-fenced ABS/receivables structure (ABS Borrower, ABS Holdings, ABS InfraCos, ABS Transaction Parties) with a Trademark License to CSC and shared-services agreements sits outside the Group Member perimeter and could be a value-transfer channel; (ii) Asset Disposition exclusions permit up to $5 million/year of dispositions and Section 1031 like-kind exchanges without falling into Section 6.08; (iii) short-dated Maturity Date (Nov 25, 2028) and high 9.00% Applicable Rate suggest stressed/rescue-financing context. No maintenance leverage covenant means limited early lender leverage absent the incurrence/Amortization Period triggers.

citation.quote: "Amortization Period" shall mean each period commencing on the first date on which the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio exceeds 2.50:1.00 and ending on the first date thereafter when the Combined Gross Pari Passu Leverage Ratio on such date does not exceed 2.50:1.00.
